_purpose: Courthouse Atlas for Johnson County, IN. Record survival for the county centered on Franklin. Well-preserved from 1823 organization. No major record losses.

previous_courthouses.fate: Brick courthouse. Served through the Civil War. Replaced by current Victorian building.

record_losses.description: Johnson County's records are well-preserved from the 1823 organization. No significant fire or flood damage to the courthouse or its records.
record_losses.narrative_impact: If {ancestor_name} lived in Johnson County, the records survive. The county's agricultural character means deed records and agricultural census schedules are particularly complete.
record_losses.source: Indiana Commission on Public Records. Guide to Indiana County Records.

deeds.repository: Johnson County Recorder; Indiana State Archives

wills_estates.repository: Johnson County Clerk; Indiana State Archives

marriages.gaps: First few years after organization may have fewer records.
marriages.repository: Johnson County Clerk; Indiana State Archives

substitute_sources.description: Franklin College (Baptist, est. 1834) maintained enrollment records, alumni records, and associated church records.
substitute_sources.genealogical_value: Enrollment records document students and their families. Baptist church records associated with the college supplement county vital records.

narrative_hooks: Johnson County's records survive complete from 1823 — {ancestor_name}'s land transactions, court appearances, and marriage record are almost certainly at the Franklin courthouse.
If {ancestor_name} attended Franklin College, the enrollment records supplement the county records with educational history.
For families displaced by the Camp Atterbury condemnation in 1942, the condemnation records at the federal courthouse document the land taken, the price paid, and the families forced to leave.
Before 1882, a missing vital record for {ancestor_name} is standard across Indiana — check church records and the census.
source_references: Indiana Commission on Public Records. Guide to Indiana County Records.
Branigin, Elba L. History of Johnson County, Indiana. Indianapolis: B.F. Bowen, 1913.
